torch.distributed.elastic.multiprocessing.errors.ChildFailedError:
Root Cause (first observed failure):

action.hypothesis: Five rounds of factorized-topk stabilization failed to approach the gated frontier, so that direction is now effectively exhausted rather than merely under-tuned. The next mandatory informative move is a fundamentally different architecture family: a `residual_topk` SAE that spends the same total K budget across two sequential sparse stages, letting a second sparse pass model the first-pass residual instead of forcing one flat support set to capture everything at once.
action.summary: Add a `residual_topk` prototype in `sparsify/` and run a K=128 proxy comparison under the current best signum regime to test whether two-stage sparse residual coding can beat flat top-k-style encoders.
action.self_review: This satisfies the runtime mandate to stop minor parameter tweaks and switch to a genuinely different architecture approach after repeated stagnation. The main risk is implementation complexity or slow runtime, but a minimal two-stage prototype is still the cleanest next discriminator because it changes the coding mechanism itself rather than repeating another local sweep.
notes_to_memory: `factorized_topk` now appears near-exhausted: EF=32 improved modestly over EF=16, but lower LR regressed and higher AUXK was effectively flat versus the best factorized result.
Severe stagnation rules out another minor parameter tweak; the next round must test a genuinely different sparse-coding mechanism.
`residual_topk` is intended as a two-stage sparse residual encoder that preserves comparable evaluation semantics while changing how the K budget is allocated internally.
next_hypotheses: If `residual_topk` runs and materially improves proxy FVU, keep the family alive and stabilize its internal K split next.
If it runs cleanly but is clearly worse than gated and not better than factorized-topk, archive it quickly and move to another architecture family.
If quality looks promising but runtime is poor, follow with a focused performance/code-fix round instead of discarding the family.
